name: Monstera obliqua — French Guiana Locality Form
aboutText: This Monstera obliqua French Guiana Locality Form is a delicate climber found in the humid, shaded understories of lowland tropical rainforests. It grows on slender trunks, anchoring itself with woody vines. The plant thrives in conditions of high, stable humidity, filtered light, and rich, decomposing organic matter. Its fenestration is a defining characteristic, making it highly sought after by collectors.

morphology.venation: Parallel, with sparse to moderate irregular fenestration

fieldNotes.title: Guiana Shield Material
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: A French Guiana locality form of Monstera obliqua — another point on the species' broad South American distribution map, here from the Guiana Shield region. At ultra rare pricing with ££££ tier, representing the most expensive category of obliqua locality forms alongside the Napo, Pastaza, and Yasuni material. The Guiana Shield flora is substantially under-collected relative to Amazonian material, which gives Guiana locality forms genuine rarity beyond simple scarcity in the trade. If authentic, buy it. Verifying authenticity is, as always with obliqua, the challenge.
